---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
df=read.csv(file.choose())
df
```
```{r}
head(df)
```
```{r}
colnames(df)
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(janitor)
# Convert date
df$order_date <- as.Date(df$order_date, format="%m/%d/%Y")
df$month <- format(df$order_date, "%Y-%m")

```
```{r}
# Monthly revenue
df$sales <- as.numeric(gsub(",", "", df$sales))
df$profit <- as.numeric(gsub(",", "", df$profit))

monthly_sales <- df %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(revenue = sum(sales, na.rm = TRUE))
monthly_sales
```
```{r}
# Top products
top_products <- df %>%
  group_by(product_name) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(sales)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total)) %>%
  head(10)

ggplot(top_products, aes(x = reorder(product_name, total), y = total)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  ggtitle("Top 10 Products")
```

```{r}
# Profit by category
profit_cat <- df %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  summarise(profit = sum(profit))

ggplot(profit_cat, aes(x = category, y = profit)) +
  geom_col() +
  ggtitle("Profit by Category")
```
